items.text: Dear Students, Our records show your meal plan has a remaining balance of $35. To receive a refund, please visit the Dining Office in Foster Hall before May 20. Refunds will be processed within five business days. Thank you, Campus Dining Services

questions.stem: According to the email, what must students do to receive a refund?

options.A: To submit a written request by email
options.B: To visit the Dining Office before May 20
options.C: To call the Dining Office during business hours
options.D: To wait five business days for processing

questions.explanation: The email states that to receive a refund, students should visit the Dining Office in Foster Hall before May 20.

questions.stem: The word "processed" in the email is closest in meaning to:
questions.explanation: In context, refunds being "processed" means they will be handled or dealt with within five business days.

items.text: Join the Spring Campus Cleanup! Volunteers needed Saturday, April 12, from 9:00 AM to noon. Meet at the Student Center lawn. Free t-shirt and lunch for all participants. A $5 deposit holds your spot and is refunded on arrival. Sign up at the Activities Office today!

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what do volunteers receive for participating?

options.B: A cash payment for their time
options.C: A parking pass for the weekend
options.D: A discount at the Student Center

questions.explanation: The flyer states that a free t-shirt and lunch are provided for all participants.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the $5 deposit?

options.A: It covers the cost of the t-shirt.
options.B: It must be paid in cash only.
options.C: It is refunded when volunteers arrive.
options.D: It reserves a spot for next year.

questions.explanation: The flyer says the deposit holds a spot and is refunded on arrival, so volunteers get it back when they show up.
